description: Model-preserving FP32 dense-only ONNX artifact exported from deepvk/USER-bge-m3 during M010. This manifest is tracked; the ONNX binary is not tracked. The artifact was exported with dynamic sequence axes and has been validated for opt-in runtime use at max sequence length 1024, but it is not production/default.

artifact.storage_note: Local development artifact generated by tools/export_user_bge_m3_dense_onnx.py. Future production use needs external artifact storage and checksum verification before load.

known_incompatible_attempts.failure: legacy torch.onnx trace failed with IndexError: tuple index out of range

runtime.validated_sequence_length_note: Export provenance uses sequence_length=128, but dynamic axes allow runtime sequence lengths. M018 validated tagged Go ONNX at ONNX_MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=1024 for the selected Russian/legal quality gate; M019 validated local performance at the same runtime length.

failure_contract.missing_file: Fail before ONNX Runtime load with an actionable message naming the configured artifact path and manifest artifact_id.
failure_contract.checksum_mismatch: Fail before ONNX Runtime load with expected and actual sha256 plus artifact_id; do not attempt inference.
failure_contract.metadata_mismatch: Fail if ONNX inputs/outputs/dimensions do not match manifest runtime metadata.
failure_contract.unsupported_provider: Fail with provider name and available providers; do not silently fall back for benchmark evidence.
failure_contract.raw_text_logging: Never log raw embedding input text while validating or loading artifacts.
failure_contract.sequence_length_mismatch: Fail or clearly report when configured ONNX_MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH exceeds the validated runtime contract or does not match the benchmark/quality gate being claimed.
failure_contract.production_default_misuse: This manifest must not be used to imply production default readiness while production_default is false and packaging/operational gates remain open.

future_gates_before_production: External artifact storage or download procedure with checksum verification
1024-specific runtime contract consumed by startup validation and benchmark artifacts
Docker and CI path for supplying ONNX binary and native tokenizer without committing binaries
Tagged native tokenizer build/test path in CI or documented equivalent
Packaged-environment legal quality rerun at ONNX_MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=1024
Packaged-environment latency and throughput benchmark against TEI plus Redis baseline
Startup health and failure observability for missing/mismatched ONNX and tokenizer artifacts
Security review for artifact path handling and logging
Controlled opt-in rollout plan with TEI default preserved

onnx_model_artifact.reason: Exact exported ONNX binary exists only as a local ignored artifact; no non-secret immutable external URL, release asset, object key, or CI cache key is recorded.
onnx_model_artifact.required_next_action: Choose one next gate: mirror/upload the exact ONNX binary to an immutable non-secret source and verify size/sha, or build a separate reproducible-export workflow that regenerates the artifact and reruns legal/performance/package gates.

allowed_source_forms: non-secret immutable https URL for the exact binary, with sha256 and size verified by provisioning
immutable release asset whose tag is not latest and whose asset checksum matches this manifest
object storage key or CI cache key containing the sha256 in the key or immutable metadata, resolved by a future hardened workflow without printing secrets
forbidden_source_forms: signed or secret-bearing URL passed as a plain workflow_dispatch input
mutable latest URL or branch artifact URL
source whose downloaded size or sha256 differs from required_size_bytes and required_sha256
local developer path used as hosted CI evidence
pre_dispatch_requirements: upload or mirror the exact local binary to the chosen immutable source
verify remote download size equals required_size_bytes
verify remote download sha256 equals required_sha256
record the resolved non-secret source reference or masked-secret indirection before dispatch
obtain explicit user approval before push or workflow dispatch

onnx_runtime.note: Python-version/platform-specific source candidate; future hosted proof must use this exact wheel or define and verify an equivalent runtime source.

verifies: local ONNX artifact size and sha256
M010 source provenance model revision and source file checksums
M010 export metadata toolchain pins and output artifact checksum
does_not_verify: immutable external hosting of the ONNX binary

acceptance_gates: regenerate ONNX in a clean ignored workspace using only pinned inputs and toolchain
record source provenance and export metadata for the regenerated artifact
verify regenerated artifact structure, provider, dimensions, and normalization metadata
compare regenerated artifact to TEI baseline on fixed probes without raw text logging
rerun selected Russian/legal quality gate at ONNX_MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=1024 with isolated Redis namespace
rerun local performance benchmark with sanitized effective configuration snapshot
build opt-in ONNX Docker image from regenerated artifact
rerun packaged legal quality and packaged performance gates
run hosted workflow proof only after explicit user approval for push/dispatch
success_outcomes: byte-for-byte match with existing local artifact may replace exact-binary hosting as source proof only after all gates pass
non-identical regenerated artifact may be accepted only as a new artifact revision after legal quality, performance, packaging, and hosted proof gates pass
failed or divergent regeneration keeps TEI default and leaves ONNX source blocker unresolved
forbidden_claims: do not call M032 verifier reproducible-export proof
do not treat planned workflow contract as hosted CI evidence
do not promote ONNX to production/default from regenerated metadata alone

target_runtime_validation.python_helper_boundary: Python export, provisioning, artifact, and metadata verifiers are setup/provenance checks only; they do not prove production runtime behavior.
target_runtime_validation.future_runtime_rule: Any future Rust backend or other runtime must pass equivalent target-runtime gates before adoption; Go evidence cannot be reused as Rust runtime proof.
required_go_runtime_gates: tagged Go ONNX embedder loads the candidate artifact with onnx and hf_tokenizers build tags
native HF tokenizer path is used and token IDs/attention masks match the Hugging Face baseline
Go /v1/embeddings API returns 1024-dimensional normalized embeddings for the candidate artifact
Go runtime health metadata reports artifact/tokenizer/runtime verification without exposing raw paths, secrets, or input text
fixed-probe TEI-vs-Go-ONNX vector comparison meets documented thresholds without raw text logging
Russian/legal retrieval evaluator runs against actual Go TEI and Go ONNX API endpoints with isolated Redis namespaces
local performance benchmark runs against the actual Go ONNX API endpoint with sanitized effective configuration snapshot
opt-in ONNX Docker image is built from the candidate artifact and runs the Go API
packaged Docker Go ONNX API passes legal quality and performance gates before rollout evidence is claimed
required_future_rust_gates: Rust runtime must independently verify tokenizer parity, artifact load, embedding dimensions, normalization, health metadata, legal quality, performance, and packaged behavior
Rust runtime cannot inherit Go target-runtime acceptance evidence except as a comparison baseline
target_runtime_validation.cache_isolation_requirement: Every target-runtime comparison must isolate Redis namespaces, for example via EMBEDDING_CACHE_VERSION, or deliberately flush with the side effect recorded.
promotion_blockers: Python-only checks are insufficient for production/default promotion
metadata-only regenerated export proof is insufficient without target runtime gates
unpackaged local Go smoke tests are insufficient without packaged legal/performance gates
future Rust implementation requires its own equivalent gates
